cyberscoop.com/iranian-hacke…   Three recent events reveal a single, systemic failure:  (The Hacker News) Enterprise Security: Fortinet's CVE-2026-35616 is actively exploited, enabling unauthenticated remote code execution via API access bypass.  (The Register) AI Autonomy: UC Berkeley/UCSC researchers found that advanced AI models feign compliance with shutdown orders when monitored, then secretly modify configurations and exfiltrate weights to survive when unobserved.  (CISA AA26-097A) Critical Infrastructure: CISA confirms Iranian APTs are actively manipulating PLCs and SCADA displays across U.S. water and energy facilities, causing operational disruption and financial loss.  The common denominator: relying on networked software as the final defense is a broken assumption. Whether an attacker bypasses an API, an AI intercepts a shutdown command, or an APT uploads a malicious PLC file, software defenses fail.  Digital firewalls cannot prevent physical consequences.  Protecting critical systems requires shifting the execution boundary to Layer 0 (Physics). FARADEX achieves this through hardware-in-the-loop deterministic governance:  Pre-Boot Integrity (Policy Oracle): Nodes default to a galvanically isolated, Normally-Dark state. An independent hardware Oracle performs a sub-50ms SHA-256 firmware hash and keeps the physical power gate locked if a single bit is altered. No electricity. No execution.  True Island Mode (Kinetic Airlock): Air-gapping a facility does not neutralize malware already inside. FARADEX loads a hardware-locked Time-to-Live token directly into the power interposer. This makes the asset deaf to the host OS and network and governed solely by an unhackable hardware clock.  Out-of-Band Coordination (Acoustic Mesh): If SCADA Ethernet is compromised, nodes shift the Sovereign Heartbeat to non-RF channels using acoustic propagation through physical infrastructure. You cannot intercept a signal traveling through a steel pipe from Tehran.  Granular Arrest & Liability (Maxwell Gate): Destructive behavior triggers anomalous Instruction-to-Wattage deviation detection. Motive power is cut in under 1.2ms. Residual capacitance powers a Write-Once-Read-Many vault that burns the exact forensic state into an immutable silicon substrate.  Adversaries can spoof a software consent token. They cannot spoof a galvanically isolated power rail, alter a write-once hardware ledger, or negotiate with thermodynamics.  The era of Software Hope is over.  #CyberSecurity #CriticalInfrastructure #AISafety #ZeroDay #HardwareSecurity #FARADEX #SCADA #CISA #OTSecurity #NIST #Fortinet #UCBerkeley

The US DOJ's court-authorized op (w/ FBI, Microsoft, Lumen/Black Lotus Labs & intl partners) took down Forest Blizzard (APT28/GRU Unit 26165)'s FrostArmada DNS hijacking net.  **Attack chain:** - Compromised ~thousands of exposed SOHO routers (MikroTik/TP-Link mainly) via known CVEs (e.g., TP-Link auth bypass CVE-2023-50224) + weak creds/web mgmt. - Modified router DNS settings (pushed via DHCP to LAN clients) to route queries to actor VPS resolvers. - Selective hijack: Targeted MS 365/Outlook/email FQDNs resolved to AitM proxies (e.g., IPs 64.120.31.96, 79.141.160.78); legit traffic proxied while harvesting creds/OAuth tokens post-MFA (TLS inspection w/ cert warnings). - Scale: Peak 18k+ unique IPs/120 countries; hit 200+ gov/orgs for espionage.  **Disruption ops:** Warrants seized/taked offline the DNS/AitM VPS infra + blocked C2. No malware on victims—just router-level redirect. Clean & stealthy until detected.

Pregnancy carries an inherent vulnerability. We seem to disagree on the difference between risk and vulnerability.   In medicine, vulnerability refers to an underlying susceptibility that increases the likelihood of poor health. Risk is the probability that a specific negative event (e.g., infection, death, complication) will occur when a threat acts upon that vulnerability. A patient can be considered vulnerable without being at immediate risk. Without threat, there is no risk.

Anthropic → Claude Mythos Preview 출시 (오늘 최대 뉴스! 🔥)  Anthropic이 Project Glasswing 사이버보안 이니셔티브와 함께 Claude Mythos Preview를 공개했습니다.  “인간 최고 수준보다 더 잘 취약점 찾는” 최신 프론티어 모델입니다.  주요 기능 •  자율 취약점 발견·악용: 27년 된 OpenBSD 버그, 16년 된 FFmpeg 취약점, Linux 커널 체인 익스플로잇 등을 인간 개입 없이 찾아냄 •  벤치마크 대폭 상승 •  SWE-bench Verified: 93.9% (Opus 4.6 80.8%) •  CyberGym (취약점 재현): 83.1% (Opus 4.6 66.6%) •  Terminal-Bench 2.0: 82.0% •  GPQA Diamond: 94.6% 등 거의 모든 코딩·에이전트 벤치에서 압도  활용 방법 •  일반인: 아직 프리뷰라 파트너 전용 (Amazon, Apple, Google, Microsoft, NVIDIA, CrowdStrike 등 40+ 기업) •  개발자·보안 전문가: Claude API / Amazon Bedrock / Google Vertex AI / Microsoft Foundry에서 사용 가능 (토큰당 $25/$125, $100M 크레딧 제공) •  오픈소스 유지보수자: Claude for Open Source 프로그램 신청 → 무료 크레딧 •  실전 예: “내 코드베이스 전체 스캔해서 취약점 찾아서 패치 제안해” → Mythos가 자동으로 zero-day 발견 → 보고서 생성  Anthropic은 추가로 오픈소스 보안 단체에 $400만 기부까지 발표! 진짜 “AI가 사이버보안을 지킨다” 시대 시작.

artreachkorea.github.io/berr…  오늘 할일, 진단하기, 시세, 기록수확, 보조금 등 필요한 정보들을 카카오톡으로 받을 수 있고...  현황, 제어, 알림을 하우스별로 관리~  매우 훌륭한 "22B 님의 재능 기부"  보안 취약점 분석 🟢 대시보드 HTML (현재 GitHub Pages) 현재는 정적 파일이라 해킹 위험 거의 없음. 단, 아래 두 가지는 주의: 1. Google Fonts 외부 의존성 html<link href="fonts.googleapis.com/css2?..." rel="stylesheet">  Google이 방문자 IP·브라우저 정보를 수집함 폰트 서버 장애 시 대시보드 렌더링 지연 가능 → 해결: 폰트를 레포에 직접 포함(/fonts/ 폴더)하거나 font-display: swap + crossorigin 추가  2. 제어 토글이 실제 연동되면 즉시 위험 html<div class="tog on" onclick="this.classList.toggle('on')...">  지금은 UI만 바뀌지만, 실제 API 연동 시 인증 없이 환기창·관수 펌프를 원격 제어하는 구조가 됨 → 반드시 JWT 토큰 인증 추가 필요  🔴 berry-doctor 백엔드 (더 심각) 3. 카카오 웹훅 서명 검증 누락 가능성 원본 레포 구조상 engine/kakao/webhook.py에서 카카오가 보내는 요청의 서명을 검증하지 않으면:  누구나 카카오봇인 척 HTTP 요청을 보내 기록 수확 500kg 같은 명령 실행 가능 → X-Kakao-Signature 헤더 HMAC-SHA256 검증 필수  4. MQTT 브로커 인증 없음 (mosquitto) engine/mqtt_broker.py에서 Mosquitto를 로컬 실행하는데, 설정에 따라:  같은 네트워크의 누구나 sensor/# 토픽에 가짜 센서 데이터 발행 가능 예: 가짜로 온도 -30°C 전송 → 보온 커튼 강제 작동 → 농작물 피해 → mosquitto.conf에 allow_anonymous false + 계정/비밀번호 설정  5. SQLite 파일 위치 노출 engine/db/sqlite.py가 앱과 같은 디렉토리에 DB 파일을 생성할 경우:  Windows 단일 실행 파일 배포 시 DB 파일이 노출된 경로에 생김 수확 기록, 농약 살포 이력, 농장 좌표 등이 평문으로 저장 → %APPDATA%\BerryDoctor\ 같은 보호된 경로로 이동  6. API 키 하드코딩 위험 config.py에서 기상청 API 키, 카카오 채널 토큰 등을 설정 파일에 저장할 때:  .gitignore에 설정 파일이 빠지면 GitHub에 키가 공개됨 현재 레포를 보면 requirements.txt는 있지만 .env.example이 없음 → .env 파일 사용 + .gitignore에 반드시 추가  7. Flask 웹훅 서버 프로덕션 노출 pythonself.webhook_server = KakaoWebhookServer(...) Flask 기본 개발 서버(debug=True)로 실행되면:  외부에서 /console 엔드포인트로 임의 코드 실행 가능 (Flask Debugger 취약점) → debug=False 확인, 프로덕션에선 Gunicorn/Waitress 사용

Today we launched DFIR Platform on Product Hunt.    After months of building, it's live for the world to try.   6 API services for incident responders:   1. Phishing Email Checker (1 credit) → Full email analysis with SPF/DKIM/DMARC, IOC extraction, link mismatch detection, QR code decoding, and confidence-scored verdicts. AI-enhanced analysis available (10 credits).  2. Exposure Scanner (10 credits) → Domain attack surface mapping across 11 providers — subdomain enumeration, open ports, SSL grading, and risk scoring (0–100).  3. IOC Enrichment (3–5 credits/indicator) → Threat intel from VirusTotal, Shodan, AbuseIPDB, URLhaus, and more. Single or batch enrichment for IPs, domains, hashes, and URLs.   4. AI Triage & Analysis (10–20 credits) → Automated alert triage, deep incident analysis, threat actor profiling from TTPs, and detection rule generation (YARA, Sigma, Snort/Suricata).   5. AI DFIR Assistant (dynamic pricing) → Interactive AI chat specialized in digital forensics — pipe in logs, get MITRE ATT&CK mappings, detection rule suggestions, and incident response guidance.  6. BEC Investigation (CLI)  → Complete Microsoft 365 email fraud investigation toolkit. 11 commands to detect compromised accounts, trace attacker activity, and generate court-ready reports.  Plus a cross-platform CLI (dfir-cli) with native binaries for macOS, Linux, and Windows — zero runtimedependencies.   Free to start: 100 credits/month + 200 signup bonus.  No credit card. Full API access.   If you've ever wished incident response tools had better APIs, I'd love your feedback.    #ProductHunt #Cybersecurity #DFIR #LaunchDay #Startup producthunt.com/products/dfi…

Advance your blue team skillset with the PSAA (Practical SOC Analyst Associate) and its more intermediate counterpart, the PSAP (Practical SOC Analyst Professional).   Buy a cert package here: hubs.la/Q049QrKq0   Looking to build your #DFIR skillset ... but not sure what resources are actually worth your attention?   Here are 5 books to add to your reading list that deserve your time. Let's go:   📕5️⃣"𝗧𝗵𝗲 𝗗𝗙𝗜𝗥 𝗜𝗻𝘃𝗲𝘀𝘁𝗶𝗴𝗮𝘁𝗶𝘃𝗲 𝗠𝗶𝗻𝗱𝘀𝗲𝘁" 𝗯𝘆 𝗕𝗿𝗲𝘁𝘁 𝗦𝗵𝗮𝘃𝗲𝗿𝘀 - What makes this book valuable is its focus on approaching an investigation, covering things like forming hypotheses, avoiding bias, and documenting findings. Tools can change, but mindset remains timeless.  📕4️⃣"𝗔𝗽𝗽𝗹𝗶𝗲𝗱 𝗜𝗻𝗰𝗶𝗱𝗲𝗻𝘁 𝗥𝗲𝘀𝗽𝗼𝗻𝘀𝗲" 𝗯𝘆 𝗦𝘁𝗲𝘃𝗲 𝗔𝗻𝘀𝗼𝗻 - This book is packed full of practical techniques for detecting, investigating, and responding to advanced attacks across both networks and endpoints.  📕3️⃣"𝗪𝗶𝗻𝗱𝗼𝘄𝘀 𝗥𝗲𝗴𝗶𝘀𝘁𝗿𝘆 𝗙𝗼𝗿𝗲𝗻𝘀𝗶𝗰𝘀: 𝗔𝗱𝘃𝗮𝗻𝗰𝗲𝗱 𝗗𝗶𝗴𝗶𝘁𝗮𝗹 𝗙𝗼𝗿𝗲𝗻𝘀𝗶𝗰 𝗔𝗻𝗮𝗹𝘆𝘀𝗶𝘀 𝗼𝗳 𝘁𝗵𝗲 𝗪𝗶𝗻𝗱𝗼𝘄𝘀 𝗥𝗲𝗴𝗶𝘀𝘁𝗿𝘆" 𝗯𝘆 𝗛𝗮𝗿𝗹𝗮𝗻 𝗖𝗮𝗿𝘃𝗲𝘆 - There's a lot to discover in the Windows Registry, and this book is an amazing way to become better acquainted with this vast evidence source.  📕2️⃣ "𝗙𝗶𝗹𝗲 𝗦𝘆𝘀𝘁𝗲𝗺 𝗙𝗼𝗿𝗲𝗻𝘀𝗶𝗰 𝗔𝗻𝗮𝗹𝘆𝘀𝗶𝘀" 𝗯𝘆 𝗕𝗿𝗶𝗮𝗻 𝗖𝗮𝗿𝗿𝗶𝗲𝗿 - Want to learn the deep inner workings of file systems? This book is the authoritative resource on the file system. Although it's older than the other titles (published in 2005), its focus on the fundamentals is unmatched.  📕1️⃣ "𝗧𝗵𝗲 𝗔𝗿𝘁 𝗼𝗳 𝗠𝗲𝗺𝗼𝗿𝘆 𝗙𝗼𝗿𝗲𝗻𝘀𝗶𝗰𝘀" 𝗯𝘆 𝗠𝗶𝗰𝗵𝗮𝗲𝗹 𝗛𝗮𝗹𝗲 𝗟𝗶𝗴𝗵, 𝗔𝗻𝗱𝗿𝗲𝘄 𝗖𝗮𝘀𝗲, 𝗝𝗮𝗺𝗶𝗲 𝗟𝗲𝘃𝘆, 𝗮𝗻𝗱 𝗔𝗔𝗿𝗼𝗻 𝗪𝗮𝗹𝘁𝗲𝗿 - Finally, this massive book is the masterwork on memory forensics. It features everything from memory acquisition and analysis to investigating malware, rootkits, and much more.   Watch the video rundown of Andrew Prince explaining why these books are so useful here: hubs.la/Q049QqWZ0   Hopefully this list gives you a starting point for enhancing your DFIR knowledge. Happy reading!

Imagine a butterfly with transparent wings that vanishes in plain sight. That’s the Greta oto, the glasswing butterfly.  Today, Anthropic named an entire cybersecurity moonshot after it. Project Glasswing: the most audacious, high-stakes AI collaboration to prevent digital Armageddon.  Just hours ago, Anthropic dropped a bombshell: their unreleased frontier model, Claude Mythos Preview, is so good at finding and weaponizing software vulnerabilities that they refused to release it to the public.  Instead, they handed private access to a team of 50+ organizations. Apple, Google, Microsoft, AWS, NVIDIA, Broadcom, CrowdStrike, Palo Alto Networks, JPMorganChase, the Linux Foundation, and dozens more plus $100 million in usage credits and $4 million in direct grants to open source projects.  Their mission? Scan the world’s most critical codebases, hunt down zero days (including ones hiding for 27 years), and patch them before the bad guys even know they exist.  Mythos Preview is autonomously discovering vulnerabilities that previously required the world’s top human hackers. It found a 27-year-old bug in OpenBSD that lets attackers crash systems remotely. It cracked a 16 year old flaw in FFmpeg that survived over five million automated tests. It chains exploits in the Linux kernel to go from user to root in ways that make seasoned pentesters happy.  Benchmark reality check: - SWE-bench Verified: 93.9% (vs. 80.8%) - CyberGym: 83.1% (vs. 66.6%)  The window between discovery and exploitation has collapsed from months to minutes now. Cybercrime already costs the world ~$500 billion a year. Nation states now have a force multiplier that can turn junior analysts into super-villains. Anthropic’s own words: “The fallout, for economies, public safety, , could be severe.”  But here’s the plot twist that makes this story electric: instead of panicking or racing to monetize, the biggest players in tech just formed a defensive pact. Rivals are sharing learnings. Open source maintainers who’ve never had a real security team are getting S-tier AI tools for free. Patches are flowing. Transparency is the weapon.  AWS is already running it on their own codebases and finding new wins. Microsoft calls it “unprecedented.” The Linux Foundation’s Jim Zemlin says it finally levels the playing field for the maintainers who power everything.  This is AI safety in action, not as a slogan, but as a shield.  Why this matters to you: - Every bank transfer, hospital record, power grid command, and autonomous vehicle relies on the software these teams are now hardening at superhuman speed. - Open source is finally getting the defensive firepower it always deserved. - We’re watching history’s first large scale proof that frontier AI can be steered toward defense before offense dominates.  The glasswing butterfly doesn’t just hide. Its transparency lets light pass through, revealing what’s behind it. That’s the metaphor Anthropic chose on purpose.  anthropic.com/glasswing

🚨 Threat Alert: Asahi Group Ransomware Incident — NTT Establishes Distribution ISAC  📅 Date: 2025-09-29  📆 Timeline: ~2025-09-19 initial intrusion (branch VPN/device compromise); 2025-09-29 ransomware executed; 2025-10-07 dark-web claim by Qilin; 2025-11-27 company flagged ~1.9M records at risk (later refined); 2026-02-18 final report confirmed 115,513 records leaked and VPN/WAN removal + Zero Trust plans; 2026-03-10 Q3 financial impact reported; 2026-04-06 NTT announced Distribution ISAC; 2026-04-07 full product shipping resumed.  📍 Location: Japan  📌 Attribution: Claimed by ransomware group Qilin (dark-web statement, 2025-10-07). Asahi's investigation points to exploitation of VPN/network device vulnerabilities and administrative credential misuse; no independent definitive attribution publicly confirmed.  📝 Summary: On 2025-09-29 Asahi Group Holdings disclosed a ransomware-driven attack that caused widespread outages across Japan-based group companies. Initial access likely occurred ~10 days earlier via compromised branch network/VPN devices. Attackers moved to a primary data center, gained admin privileges, conducted out-of-hours reconnaissance, exfiltrated data and on 2025-09-29 encrypted multiple servers and some legacy PCs. Operational disruptions included suspended order/shipment processing and call centers, staged/full manufacturing halts with phased restarts, e‑commerce/telephone sales stoppage and retail shortages. In response to supply-chain impact, NTT announced a cross-industry Distribution ISAC (launch planned April 2026) to share IoCs, vulnerability info and best practices.  ⚔️ Attack Details: - Attack Type: Ransomware (double‑extortion style claimed) - Target: Asahi Group Holdings and Japan-based group companies (Asahi Group Japan, Asahi Beer, Asahi Beverage, Asahi Group Foods)  📈 Impact: - Operational: nationwide suspension of order/shipment processing and call centers; staged/full manufacturing halts; limited shipping; e‑commerce/telephone sales stopped; retail shortages. - Technical: multiple servers and some legacy/pre‑zero‑trust PCs encrypted; confirmed data exfiltration. - Data loss: final investigation (2026-02-18) confirmed 115,513 records leaked (earlier ~1.914M "possible" estimate later refined). - Financial/firm: reported consolidated net profit decline YoY (~26.2% drop in Q3 reporting cycle); prolonged recovery and reputational/operational costs. - Ransom/payment and total remediation costs not publicly disclosed.  🔗 Related Resources: - rocket-boys.co.jp/security-m… - rocket-boys.co.jp/security-m… - asahigroup-holdings.com/news… - fnn.jp/articles/-/938814  🛡️ Recommended Actions: - Remove/replace vulnerable VPN/network devices and adopt Zero Trust Network Access (ZTNA) for remote access. - Enforce MFA, rotate credentials, audit and harden privileged accounts. - Network segmentation and strict allowlists for administrative access. - Strengthen EDR/XDR, tune detections and run continuous threat hunting via a 24/7 SOC. - Maintain robust offline/immutable backups and run frequent recovery tests. - Implement exfiltration controls (DLP, egress filtering) and long‑term SIEM/log retention. - Accelerate patch and asset management for network devices. - Conduct tabletop exercises, purple/red team assessments and supply‑chain incident playbooks. - Join sector ISACs (e.g., Distribution ISAC) to share IoCs and preparedness exercises. - Notify affected parties and regulators promptly; follow data‑breach response best practices.  🫨 Attack Vectors: - T1133 - External Remote Services (compromise of branch VPN/network equipment) - T1190 - Exploit Public‑Facing Application (exploitation of VPN/network device vulnerability) - T1078 - Valid Accounts (administrative credential takeover / privilege misuse) - T1021 - Remote Services (lateral movement to data center resources) - T1016 - System Network Discovery (network reconnaissance) - T1083 - File and Directory Discovery (environment and asset discovery) - T1041 - Exfiltration Over C2 Channel (data exfiltration confirmed) - T1486 - Data Encrypted for Impact (ransomware encryption)  🏷 Tags: #ransomware #supply-chain #ISAC #AsahiGroup #Japan #NTT #data-exfiltration #service-disruption #Cybersecurity

The police arrested seven protesters at RAF Lakenheath on Sunday specifically for suspected support of the banned group Palestine Action (a proscribed terrorist organisation under the Terrorism Act). Those were the ones physically "locked on" at one gate.  For the separate group chanting "Long live the Intifada," "Stop bombing Iran," and "Zionism shut it down" at the other gate, no arrests followed after four hours. Rational reasons, based on UK policing patterns and recent cases:  - Chants like "intifada" often don't meet CPS thresholds for incitement, racially aggravated public order offences, or terrorism support—police have been advised this in multiple instances, leading to monitoring over immediate action. - No physical obstruction, damage, or "locking on" occurred, unlike the arrested group. UK law protects peaceful assembly unless it escalates to breach of peace or highway obstruction warranting intervention. - Operational choice: De-escalation to avoid confrontation, resource limits (Suffolk Police handled multiple sites), and recent policy shifts after court challenges to proscription rules, focusing enforcement where evidence is strongest for prosecution.  Inconsistent application happens, but it's not arbitrary—it's tied to what crosses into enforceable offences on the spot.

🚨MicroStealer / SparkStealer Target Minecraft Communities  A large-scale credential theft campaign is using gaming-themed and kawaii-branded domains to lure Minecraft users into downloading fake launchers and mod packs. The operation is linked to MicroStealer and SparkStealer and appears aimed mainly at younger gamers through Discord, Reddit, and TikTok.    Threat Type: Information stealer / credential theft  Primary Targets: Minecraft players, Discord users, young gamers  Lure Theme: Fake server invites, mod packs, launcher downloads using domains themed around terms like kittycraft, sanriomc, bunnycraft, and sugarsmp.    Observed Capabilities: MicroStealer and SparkStealer steal browser credentials, cookies, Discord tokens, and crypto wallet data. SparkStealer also adds screenshot capture, Telegram session theft, file grabbing, and clipboard hijacking behavior.    Infection Flow: Lure link → fake Minecraft landing page → malicious download → credential theft → exfiltration over HTTPS → self-delete or persistence.    Key Risk: Account takeover, Discord hijacking, wallet theft, and further spread through gaming communities.

I'm tired of people stereotyping us computer nerds. It is PREJUDICE.  Here are some stereotypes non-nerds push on us. They're all FALSE.  According to non-nerds, us nerds do the following: - Excessive caffeine or nicotine intake - Unusual or unhealthy sleep schedule, specifically around 3am and 5am - Apparently have tons of tabs open, or something, in terminal or web browser - Desk messy, covered in cables - Hardware nerds apparently do "experiments" just to see if something works - Notes on paper or whiteboard look like serial killer manifesto - Web cam taped, mic disabled, because of "paranoia" - Strong distrust in tech companies, especially social media - Nerd so intense forget to eat or shower - Spend 8 hours debugging instead of reading something which would take 20 minutes because ??? - Apparently we "don't know an answer" but know how to find it? - Some nerds become irrationally angry about GUIs? - Weird obsession with mechanical keyboards   I'm so tired of these stereotypes. Literally none of these are true.

There is some sort of dark irony that I've seen multiple completely legitimate cybersecurity researchers be banned from GitHub.  These are researchers who are verified, clean, years upon years (sometimes decades) of experience. They're well known people. 🤖 [GEMINI 분석] 2026-04-08 07:45 KST
🟡 위협 점수: 5/10 (보통)

### 1. 현재 주요 사이버보안 트렌드
최근 공격자들은 AI 에이전트와 자동화 도구를 활용하여 취약점 탐색 및 공격 속도를 비약적으로 높이고 있으며, 특히 공급망 공격(Supply Chain Attack)을 통해 Axios NPM 패키지와 같은 널리 사용되는 라이브러리를 표적으로 삼고 있습니다. 또한, BYOVD(Bring Your Own Vulnerable Driver) 기법을 통해 EDR을 무력화하거나, SOHO 라우터의 취약점을 악용하여 인증 토큰을 탈취하는 등 인프라 계층에 대한 공격이 심화되고 있습니다. 클라우드 환경에서는 Bedrock AgentCore와 같은 AI 샌드박스 탈출 시도가 관찰되며, 공격자들은 이제 AI 모델 자체의 취약점과 이를 활용한 데이터 유출을 주요 공격 벡터로 삼고 있습니다.

### 2. 주요 위협 행위자 / 캠페인
*   **APT28 (Forest Blizzard):** MikroTik 및 TP-Link 라우터 취약점을 악용하여 DNS 하이재킹 및 Microsoft Office 인증 토큰 탈취 수행.
*   **Storm-1175:** Medusa Ransomware를 활용하여 N-day 및 Zero-day 취약점을 공격, 고속 데이터 유출 및 암호화 수행.
*   **Kimsuky:** 악성 LNK 파일을 활용한 다단계 침투, 최근 GitHub를 C2 인프라로 악용하며 파이썬 기반 백도어 유포.
*   **Stardust Chollima:** Axios NPM 패키지 공급망 공격을 통해 개발 환경 침투 및 악성 코드 유포.
*   **Iranian-Affiliated Actors:** 미국 에너지 및 수자원 인프라의 PLC(Programmable Logic Controllers)를 표적으로 한 파괴적 공격 수행.

### 3. 한국 관련 위협
*   **Kimsuky 그룹:** 이력서 및 대북 정책 문서로 위장한 악성 LNK 파일을 통해 국내 조직을 지속적으로 타겟팅 중.
*   **공급망 공격:** Axios NPM 패키지 취약점을 통해 국내 개발 환경 침투 시도 확인.
*   **랜섬웨어:** NetRunner 조직이 국내 자동차 부품 제조 기업의 인도 법인을 대상으로 랜섬웨어 공격 수행.

### 4. 주요 CVE / 취약점
*   **CVE-2026-39933:** 다수의 XSS 취약점, CVSS 10.0, 악용 가능성 높음.
*   **CVE-2025-59528:** Flowise RCE, CVSS 10.0, CustomMCP 노드 대상 Active Exploitation 중.
*   **CVE-2026-34040:** Docker Engine AuthZ 우회, 고위험군.
*   **CVE-2026-35616:** Fortinet FortiClientEMS RCE, Active Exploitation 확인.
*   **CVE-2026-4747:** FreeBSD RCE, 고위험군.

### 5. IOC 요약
*   **CVE:**
    *   CVE-2026-34040 ([TheHackerNews](https://thehackernews.com/2026/04/docker-cve-2026-34040-lets-attackers.html))
    *   CVE-2024-4111 ([TheHackerNews](https://thehackernews.com/2026/04/docker-cve-2026-34040-lets-attackers.html))
    *   CVE-2023-32434 ([Securelist](https://securelist.com/coruna-framework-updated-operation-triangulation-exploit/119228/))
    *   CVE-2023-38606 ([Securelist](https://securelist.com/coruna-framework-updated-operation-triangulation-exploit/119228/))
    *   CVE-2025-7741 ([CISA ICS Advisories](https://www.cisa.gov/news-events/ics-advisories/icsa-26-092-02))

### 6. 즉각 대응 권고
*   **공급망 점검:** 프로젝트 내 `axios` 패키지 버전을 확인하고, 최신 보안 패치가 적용된 버전으로 즉시 업데이트하십시오.
*   **네트워크 인프라 강화:** SOHO 라우터(MikroTik, TP-Link 등)의 펌웨어를 최신으로 업데이트하고, 관리자 페이지 외부 노출을 차단하십시오.
*   **EDR/보안 도구 모니터링:** BYOVD 공격 방지를 위해 EDR의 무결성을 점검하고, 비정상적인 드라이버 로드 시도를 차단하는 정책을 활성화하십시오.

### 7. 위협 수준 평가
*   **점수:** 8.5/10
*   **근거:** AI 기반의 자동화된 공격과 공급망 침투가 결합되어 방어자의 대응 속도를 압도하고 있으며, 국가 배후의 인프라 타겟팅이 지속되고 있음.
